page: Amazon Web Services m5a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, including single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, falls into the bottom 10% tier. However, the high core count enables average multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with both cached and uncached read-only operations ranking in the top 25%. Cryptographic performance is also strong, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instructions, while database operations, static web serving, and Redis SET operations perform at average levels.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5a.24xlarge leverages AMD EPYC processors to offer a dense allocation of resources for multi-threaded workloads without the premium associated with higher single-core clock speeds. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, memory-bandwidth-sensitive tasks, static web serving, and database operations that can leverage the 384.0 GB memory footprint. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM text generation, where its performance is comparatively weak.
description: Amazon Web Services m5a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and AMD EPYC 7571 processors. It features 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps without local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks reveal a contrast between poor single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities, alongside strong memory bandwidth and cryptographic execution. Qualitatively, this instance provides a resource-dense option for multi-threaded workloads, making it suitable for database operations, static web serving, and memory-intensive applications that do not depend on high single-thread speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and memory-bandwidth-sensitive applications.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384.0 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance featuring ninety-six virtual cores and strong memory bandwidth for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong cached and uncached read bandwidth
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong cryptographic performance with hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions
Best suited for multi-threaded databases, static web serving, and memory-intensive tasks
